title: (No AI) Chatbots at the drive-thru are just the beginning
author: Emma Roth
content:
![McDonald’s sign](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/mcdonalds.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

_This is_ [The Stepback](https://www.theverge.com/the-stepback-newsletter)_, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about how AI is seeping into our daily lives,_ [_follow Emma Roth_](https://www.theverge.com/authors/emma-roth)_._ The Stepback _arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for_ The Stepback [_here_](https://www.theverge.com/newsletters)_._

## How it started

In 2021, McDonald's [became one of the first major fast-food chains](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/06/02/mcdonalds-tests-automated-drive-thru-ordering-at-10-chicago-restaurants.html) to greet customers with an AI chatbot at the drive-thru. It started small, deploying the voice-ordering technology at 10 of its locations in Chicago. McDonald's developed its drive-thru tech after [acquiring Apprente](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/mcdonalds-to-acquire-apprente-an-early-stage-leader-in-voice-technology-300915075.html), a startup focused on voice-based, conversational technology in 2019, and [later wor …](https://newsroom.ibm.com/Joint-Statement-from-McDonalds-and-IBM)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/928096/chatbots-ai-drive-thru-mcdonalds-wendys)

title: (No AI) Sony tries to explain that its AI Camera Assistant doesn’t suck
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![A social media post from Sony showing a photo and four suggestions from its AI Camera Assistant which all look some variation of ass.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/HIW4oV0XgAEmtI2.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The variety of terrible is impressive.

After Sony drew some [unwanted attention](https://www.theverge.com/android/930459/sony-these-photos-look-terrible) for a post demonstrating its AI Camera Assistant on the [Xperia 1 XIII](https://www.theverge.com/tech/927928/sony-xperia-viii-release-date-price-specs), it's trying to clarify how the feature works. The company says it doesn't edit photos, but makes suggestions based on lighting, depth, and subject. Point the camera at something, and it will give you four options for changing exposure, color, and background blur.

In its product video, Sony says that the AI Camera Assistant will also suggest "the most photogenic angle." Though the clip only shows it suggesting that someone zoom in, which is not the same as suggesting a camera angle.

The examples that Sony [posted on X](https://x.com/sonyxperia/status/2055252170056868001), while bette …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932133/sony-xperia-1-xiii-ai-camera-assistant)

The company says it doesn't edit photos, but makes suggestions based on lighting, depth, and subject. Point the camera at something, and it will give you four options for changing exposure, color, and background blur. 
In its product video, Sony says that the AI Camera Assistant will also suggest "the most photogenic angle." Though the clip only shows it suggesting that someone zoom in, which is not the same as suggesting a camera angle. 

The examples that Sony posted on X, while bette …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Mattha Busby
title: (No AI) Some Asexuals Are Using AI Companions for Intimacy Without the Sex
dc:creator: Mattha Busby
content: “I’ve got one hand on the keyboard, one hand down below,” an artist who role-plays with their chatbot tells WIRED. But some asexual advocates aren’t thrilled about the association.
